instructions:
Extend the local CI gate to also run Android doc-as-test (4th check alongside regex drift + TS + Flutter doc-as-test). Mirror exactly what 0055 did for Flutter — same shape, same pattern.

## Part A — Gitignore preempt

Proactively add Android generated artifacts to `.gitignore` BEFORE wiring the gate, to avoid the leak that happened with Flutter:

```
# Android doc-as-test generated files
.docs-ops/integration-tests/android/results/extracted/*.kt
.docs-ops/integration-tests/android/results/extracted/*.java
.docs-ops/integration-tests/android/results/extracted/_manifest.json
.docs-ops/integration-tests/android/.gradle/
.docs-ops/integration-tests/android/build/
.docs-ops/integration-tests/android/local.properties
```

The 0056 commit may have already leaked some — if so, untrack them via `git rm -rf --cached` and ensure `.docs-ops/integration-tests/android/results/extracted/.gitkeep` is preserved so the directory persists in fresh clones.

## Part B — Extend `check-drift.py` with Android check

Add `run_android_doc_as_test_check()` parallel to the existing TS and Flutter functions. Same shape: skip-if-unavailable, set SP_SDKS_ROOT env, invoke extractor + runner, read latest.json, classify severity. Return same schema as the other two.

In `main()`, after the Flutter check, call the Android check. Combined verdict: ANY blocking failure on any of TS / Flutter / Android fails the gate.

Update the printed summary to show all four signals symmetrically:

```
  Regex drift:           baseline=0  candidate=0  delta=0  ✓
  Doc-as-test (TS):       passed=72  skipped=4  warnings=0  blocking=0  ✓
  Doc-as-test (Flutter):  passed=41  skipped=3  warnings=0  blocking=0  ✓
  Doc-as-test (Android):  passed=70  skipped=9  warnings=4  blocking=0  ✓
  PASS — okay to push.
```

Extend `--quiet` one-liner with `an_*` fields:

```
docs-ops drift: baseline=0 candidate=0 delta=0 new_pairs=0 ts_passed=72 ts_skipped=4 ts_blocking=0 fl_passed=41 fl_skipped=3 fl_blocking=0 an_passed=70 an_skipped=9 an_blocking=0 verdict=PASS
```

## Part C — Update CI_GATE.md

Update the Doc-as-test section: three languages covered (TS + Flutter + Android), same gate policy applies per platform, per-platform scope notes. Add a line in the 'Scope' subsection: iOS is the remaining platform.

## Verification

After the changes, run `python3 .docs-ops/ci/check-drift.py --base HEAD --skip-fetch`. Verify:
  - Output shows ALL FOUR signals (Regex, TS, Flutter, Android)
  - Each green (drift=0, all doc-as-test platforms pass+skip 100%)
  - Exit code 0
  - The `--quiet` one-liner shows all `ts_*`, `fl_*`, `an_*` fields
  - `.gitignore` covers Android extracted artifacts (verify `git status` clean after a framework re-run)

Report in `notes`: confirmation gitignore is clean; output of a successful gate run; any quirks (e.g., if `kotlinc` isn't installed on the system, runner crash should be reported as 'unavailable', not as a blocking failure).

validators.name: gate_runs_all_four_checks_and_gitignore_clean
validators.command: python3 .docs-ops/ci/check-drift.py --base HEAD --skip-fetch 2>&1 | tee /tmp/gate.out | tail -12 && grep -q 'Android' /tmp/gate.out && grep -q 'Flutter' /tmp/gate.out && grep -q 'PASS' /tmp/gate.out && grep -q 'android/results/extracted/\*\.kt' .gitignore && echo OK
